title: Session Stack Walkthrough
description: Walk the VTAM -> TSO -> ISPF layers and learn where trust boundaries shift.

steps.title: VTAM Logon
steps.screen:
WELCOME TO VTAM
APPLID ===> TSO

Enter the application ID to connect.
steps.expected: Identify the VTAM boundary and the application ID prompt.

steps.title: TSO Logon
steps.screen:
TSO/E LOGON
User ID  ===>
Password ===>

Enter credentials to establish a TSO session.
steps.hint: Logon credentials establish the user context; this is not SSH.

steps.title: ISPF Primary Menu
steps.screen:
ISPF Primary Option Menu
Option ===>

0  Settings
1  View
2  Edit
3  Utilities
5  Batch
6  Command

F1=Help F3=Exit F4=Return
steps.expected: Note the subsystem boundary between TSO and ISPF.
steps.hint: ISPF is a program running under TSO; note the menu-driven access model.

steps.title: Batch Boundary
steps.screen:
Submit batch job from ISPF option 5.
You are moving from interactive to deferred execution.
steps.expected: Explain why batch introduces delayed execution and different trust controls.
steps.hint: Batch runs under JES with different scheduling and authority checks.
